items.headline: Iran fires on Israel and Gulf neighbors as Trump claims threat from Tehran nearly eliminated
items.summary: Iran's attacks on Gulf states along with its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz have disrupted the world's energy supplies with effects far beyond the Middle East. That has proved to be Iran's greatest strategic advantage in the war.

items.headline: UK Hosts 40-Nation Talks to Reopen Iran-Blocked Strait of Hormuz as Oil Prices Surge Amid War
items.summary: LONDON — Britain convened a virtual summit Thursday with about 40 countries to explore ways of reopening the Strait of Hormuz, the vital Persian Gulf chokepoint effectively shut down by Iran during the ongoing U.S.-Israeli war, as global oil prices climbed on…

items.headline: Pakistan to continue with Iran-US mediation despite ‘obstacles’
items.summary: Islamabad's statement follows Trump threatening to bomb Iran 'back to the Stone Ages'.

items.headline: US military vs Iran's 'arch defence': Dangerous new phase of war looms
items.summary: While Donald Trump talks about ending and winning the war in Iran, major US military assets are still on their way to the Middle East. These are the options they give him.

items.headline: The Critical Commodities Caught in the Hormuz Blockade
items.summary: When an Indian tanker carrying liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) passed through the Strait of Hormuz recently, its progress was followed by excited live TV news coverage. “Its position was received eight minutes ago. It’s currently at 12.5 knots and is moving at …

items.headline: Oil Spikes As Trump Vows To Hit Iran “Extremely Hard Over Next 2-3 Weeks”
items.summary: Trump declares 'core strategic objectives met', threatens 2-3 more weeks of bombing, no mention of ceasefire

items.headline: What is Benjamin Netanyahu’s end game in the Iran war?
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is a huge political book for the Israeli prime minister in an election year – but it may come at a great cost.

items.headline: Putin Is Not Trapped: Why Regime Survival Does Not Depend on Victory
items.summary: For four years, analysts and policymakers have warned that Russian President Vladimir Putin “cannot afford to lose” in Ukraine. Increasingly, some argue he cannot afford peace either. In this view, the war is existential for his regime. Defeat would shatter P…

items.headline: Press conference by NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte at the launch of the 2025 Annual Report
items.summary: Good afternoon, and thank you for joining us today for the launch of our 2025 Annual Report.

items.headline: Will China join Pakistan-led efforts to mediate US-Iran peace?
items.summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visits China after Islamabad talks to broker negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.headline: The war with Iran may be ushering in a new nuclear age
items.summary: Global tensions are rising as nations consider developing nuclear weapons. Donald Trump's policies are prompting countries like Germany and Poland to explore their own nuclear deterrents. Meanwhile, China and Russia are concerned about proliferation in Japan …

items.headline: US Burned 14 Years of Missiles in 30 Days
items.summary: The US fired 850 Tomahawk missiles in 30 days but buys only 57 a year. As munitions run dry, the Pentagon pivots to ground troops in Iran, guaranteeing the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, European gas storage hits crisis levels, and Western energy prices keep …

items.headline: Trump's Iran-war address caused another oil spike. Here's what smart people in markets are saying.
items.summary: Business Insider gathered commentary from top expects as oil prices climbed on Thursday after Trump's remarks on the war in Iran.

items.headline: Morgan Stanley analyst sends urgent S&P 500 message
items.summary: The S&P 500 just proved the bears wrong, and the worst may be over. After weeks of pressure tied to rising oil prices and escalating tensions in the Middle...

items.headline: Oil, War and the Global Economy: The Market's Narrative in March 2026
items.summary: Markets play an expectations game, and in March 2026, we saw the process play out, with all of its upsides and downsides. The month started with a war in the Middle East, which quickly percolated int… [+28762 chars]

items.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei Issues First Written Message as Supreme Leader Amid Questions Over Health
items.summary: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei released his first written statement without appearing on video. The message was read by a state television anchor while displaying a still photograph of him, raising questions about his condition following reports he may have been injured during strikes that killed his father.

dimension_signal.rationale: First statement delivered only in writing through TV anchor raises transparency concerns about leadership

items.headline: Netanyahu Expected to Visit Hungary Despite ICC Arrest Warrant
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is expected to travel to Hungary on March 21 to speak at Conservative Political Action Conference. Human Rights Watch urges Hungarian authorities to arrest him under ICC obligations, though Hungary previously failed to comply during his April 2025 visit.

dimension_signal.rationale: Deliberately avoiding ICC member states where arrest warrant would be enforced

items.headline: Pakistan's Army Chief Emerges as Key Iran-US Mediator as India Feels Marginalized
items.summary: Field Marshal Asim Munir has positioned himself as central mediator between US and Iran, delivering a 15-point US peace plan to Tehran. This diplomatic role marks a humiliating failure for Modi's foreign policy which sought to isolate Pakistan, leaving India relegated to a single phone call from Trump.

dimension_signal.rationale: Successfully positioning Pakistan as neutral mediator enhances diplomatic standing

items.headline: Taiwan Opposition Leader Accepts Xi Jinping's Invitation to Visit China
items.summary: Kuomintang chairperson Cheng Li-wun accepted an invitation from Chinese leader Xi Jinping to visit China from April 7-12. The visit comes amid tensions as Taiwan's DPP accuses Cheng of doing Beijing's bidding by stalling government defense spending plans.

dimension_signal.rationale: Engaging Taiwan opposition demonstrates continued diplomatic approach over military confrontation

items.headline: Trump Approaches 60-Day War Powers Deadline Without Clear Iran Exit Strategy
items.summary: President Trump faces the War Powers Resolution 60-day deadline while projecting confidence about Iran campaign completion. He threatens to hit Iran 'extremely hard over next 2-3 weeks' but offers no definitive end date, creating tension between promised timeline and legal requirements.

dimension_signal.rationale: Continuing military operations without clear exit strategy while approaching legal deadline

items.headline: North Korea Launches Massive Ballistic Missile Barrage Amid US-South Korea Drills
items.summary: North Korea fired 10 ballistic missiles simultaneously in an unusual large-scale test, with 600mm-caliber ultra-precision multiple rocket launchers achieving 100 percent accuracy against targets 364.4 km away. The unprecedented scale appears calculated to project power during global volatility while US resources are stretched by Iran conflict.

dimension_signal.rationale: Unusually large-scale missile test during US-Iran conflict demonstrates opportunistic escalation

items.headline: Pakistan Army Deploys 'Ghatak' Units Along India Border Amid Iran Mediation Role
items.summary: Despite Pakistan's diplomatic mediation role, General Asim Munir has deployed specialized 'killer' units along the Jammu-Rajouri border. Intelligence reports indicate Pakistani terrorist groups are planning Border Action Team attacks targeting Indian Army personnel, with three separate groups active in the LoC sector.

dimension_signal.rationale: Deploying attack units while serving as peace mediator shows contradictory military posturing

items.headline: Senate Rejects War Powers Resolution to Constrain Trump on Iran by 53-47 Vote
items.summary: The Senate rejected a war powers resolution seeking to force President Trump to get congressional consent for military actions against Iran. The vote reflects Congress becoming more of a bystander than decider on US military operations, as Trump reportedly prepares to request $50 billion in supplemental funding.

dimension_signal.rationale: Senate failure to constrain presidential war powers reduces institutional checks on executive authority

items.headline: China Increases Defense Budget 7% to $278 Billion Despite Slowing Economic Growth
items.summary: The PRC Ministry of Finance proposed a defense budget of approximately $278 billion for 2026, representing a 7% increase despite GDP growth targets falling to their lowest since 1991. Xi Jinping linked PLA modernization to party building in the military during March plenary session.

dimension_signal.rationale: Prioritizing military spending despite economic challenges shows regime security focus

items.headline: Kim Jong-un Orders Massive Missile Production Increase for 2026 Operations
items.summary: North Korean dictator Kim Jong-un ordered factories to significantly increase missile and munitions production in 2026 to meet growing demand. During visits to munitions plants, Kim instructed production sites to satisfy military requirements and ordered construction of new ammunition factories, calling the sector 'critical for military deterrence.'

dimension_signal.rationale: Massive military production increase demonstrates focus on regime security through deterrence
